rulings.Case Title: DR. DOE VS. KAISER PERMANENTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Form Interrogatories, Set Two; Supplemental Form Interrogatories; And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request To Admit Answers To Request For Admissions, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2024, line 2, DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS (ON BEHALF OF ERRONEOUSLY SUED NON-LEGAL ENTITY, KAISER PERMANENTE) Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Form Interrogatories, Set Two; Supplemental Form Interrogatories; And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request To Admit Answers To Request For Admissions, Set Two The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches a copy of the report and recommendation and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA OSTROVSKY VS. LEGALMATCH.COM, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Compensatory And Punitive Damages Claims For Plaintiff; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of John R Grele
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2024, line 12, DEFENDANTS DMITRY SHUBOV AN INDIVIDUAL, VLADIMIR SHUBOV'S Motion To Strike Compensatory And Punitive Damages Claims For Plaintiff The motion to strike is granted with 20 days leave to amend. At the last hearing involving the original complaint, the court noted that the allegations regarding the $3 million dollar transfer implicated a corporate injury and gave plaintiff leave to amend to plead a basis to compel a dividend or cite other injury "not incidental to an injury to the corporation." (Jones v. H.F. Ahmanson & Co. (1969) 1 Cal.3d 93, 107 ["If the injury is not incidental to an injury to the corporation, an individual cause of action exists."].) The first amended complaint does not plead a basis for direct recovery and plaintiff apparently now only seeks to allege derivative causes of action. But the first amended complaint still seeks to recover for a direct/personal injury to plaintiff. (First Amended Complaint, pars. 37, 39, 45, 47.) Indeed, plaintiff filed the first amended complaint as "an individual" (not a proxy/representative of the corporation) even though she is prosecuting derivative claims. (Corporations Code section 800(b) [conditions to maintain derivative action].) The adduced allegations are improper as plaintiff alleges no basis to recover direct compensatory or punitive damages. Only Legalmatch.com can recover such damages. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege the derivative claims in a proper format. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY SHAW VS. ORANGE DAO LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2024, line 6, 1 - DEFENDANTS DONALD HO , AN INDIVIDUAL, DAE HUH, ORANGE FUND 1 LLC, ORANGE DAO LLC, YC ALUM PARTNERS LLC, ORANGE FUND GP LLC'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. Defendants first say they "cannot understand which entity Plaintiff is referring to throughout the pleading" - i.e., "which defendant was responsible for what action." (Memo. 3:22-4:2.) But each FAC count begins by stating which defendants are targeted therein, and alter ego is adequately pled. (FAC 4:19-6:15.) Defendants next say they are not employers because their entities are "decentralized autonomous organizations" dealing in cryptocurrency. (Memo. 5:10-6:4.) These are issues not resolvable at the pleading stage; the FAC adequately alleges employer status. (FAC 6:16-7:7.) Breach of contract and conversion are also adequately pled. (FAC 11:15-12:3, 19:4-23.) The implied covenant of good faith is properly pled in the alternative. (See Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: JOHN CRESS VS. SPEAR INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2024, line 7, 1 - DEFENDANT MIGUEL YNCLINO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Pro Per Defendant Miguel Ynclino's motion to strike is denied. Ynclino answered the complaint on June 18, 2024. A motion to strike a complaint must be served and filed within "the time allowed to respond to" the complaint. (CCP 435(b)(1); CRC 3.1322(b).) A "defendant can move to strike a complaint only before he has answered it and not afterward." (Adohr Milk Farms, Inc. v. Love (1967) 255 Cal.App.2d 366, 371.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)
